issues.body: collect_bgps handles GraphPattern::Join identically to Union and pushes each child BGP separately. witness_abox then assigns each child a distinct di namespace, so a variable shared across the join is frozen to different IRIs and no generated data necessarily satisfies the complete joined CQ. For a query written with nested groups that parse as Join, both the raw query and a wrongly minimized query can consequently be empty over the synthesized witness. The claimed per-disjunct canonical-instance guarantee does not hold on this admitted path. The current shared-variable test places both triples in a single BGP, so it does not cover Join.
issues.fix_hint: Preserve conjunction when traversing Join: construct one canonical instance for the combined joined branch with a shared variable-freezing map (while Union creates alternatives), or fail closed on Join shapes that are not proven to be emitter structure. Add a regression whose original/raw query contains Join(Bgp, Bgp) sharing a variable and whose deliberately wrong minimized query drops a non-subsumed branch; assert the revised witness is non-empty and detects the mismatch (or refuses witness mode with no timing row).
issues.title: Join nodes are frozen as separate disjuncts, making the witness gate vacuous

summary: Round 3 fixes the prior FILTER/VALUES witness-mode defect by refusing those modifiers and adds a direct refusal regression. However, the witness traversal still treats GraphPattern::Join like Union: each joined BGP is frozen as an independent disjunct with a different per-disjunct namespace. A syntactically joined CQ can therefore have no complete canonical instance, making raw and wrongly minimized queries agree vacuously. The existing multi-atom regression uses one BGP and does not exercise this path. This is a new major finding, so progress is regressing under the required scale.
